title: AI Governance and Regulation

modules.title: Global AI Policy Landscape
modules.sub: Mapping how nations are approaching AI governance today
modules.description: Learners survey the current state of AI policy across major regions, comparing regulatory philosophies in the EU, US, China, and beyond. They assess how geopolitical priorities shape policy choices and identify key similarities and tensions between national approaches. By the end, learners can orient themselves within the global policy environment.

modules.title: Regulatory Frameworks Emerging
modules.sub: Decoding the rules and requirements taking shape worldwide
modules.description: Learners examine specific regulatory instruments—such as the EU AI Act, executive orders, and sector-specific rules—to understand their scope, obligations, and enforcement mechanisms. They practice analyzing how these frameworks apply to real AI use cases and organizational contexts. Learners leave equipped to evaluate compliance implications for a given deployment.

modules.title: Industry Self-Regulation
modules.sub: Evaluating voluntary standards, codes, and corporate commitments
modules.description: Learners investigate how companies, industry consortia, and standards bodies are developing voluntary governance mechanisms alongside formal regulation. They critically assess the strengths and limitations of self-regulatory approaches, including accountability gaps and incentive misalignments. Learners form a reasoned position on when self-regulation is sufficient and when it is not.

modules.title: Future Governance Models
modules.sub: Anticipating how AI oversight will evolve and what it demands
modules.description: Learners explore proposed and experimental governance models—including adaptive regulation, international treaties, and multi-stakeholder bodies—and evaluate their feasibility for governing rapidly advancing AI. They consider the roles that professionals, policymakers, and civil society must play in shaping these systems. Learners synthesize insights to develop a perspective on effective, forward-looking AI governance.

synopsis: Explore how governments and organizations are regulating AI development and deployment. Understand current policies and emerging governance frameworks worldwide.
